_notes: Commodity routes through and around the Persian Gulf. blockade_sensitivity = how much Hormuz closure affects this route (1.0 = fully blocked, 0 = unaffected). capacity_mbpd is pre-crisis baseline.

routes.name: Strait of Hormuz
routes.notes: 21-mile-wide strait; only maritime exit from Persian Gulf. Pre-crisis: ~17 Mbpd oil, ~25% of global LNG.

routes.name: Suez Canal
routes.notes: Alternative for oil from Gulf to Europe via Red Sea. Does not help Asia-bound Gulf cargo. Had own disruption risk from Houthi attacks 2024-25.

routes.name: Cape of Good Hope
routes.notes: Full bypass for Gulf oil. No capacity limit but adds 14-21 days and ~$3-5/barrel. Used extensively during 2024 Red Sea disruption.

routes.name: East-West Pipeline (Petroline)
routes.notes: Only Saudi crude. Destination Yanbu then Suez or direct shipping. Was underutilized pre-crisis; now critical bypass. Total capacity 2 Mbpd but ~1.2 Mbpd available beyond current use.

routes.name: Abu Dhabi-Fujairah Pipeline (ADCOP)
routes.notes: UAE crude only. Fujairah is outside the strait. Operational and most strategically significant bypass built specifically for this contingency.

routes.name: Kirkuk-Ceyhan Pipeline (Iraq-Turkey)
routes.notes: Only Kirkuk crude. Often damaged; intermittently operational even pre-crisis. Currently partially operational at ~0.2 Mbpd.
